reforms.description: The creation of the Haute École Spécialisée (University of Applied Sciences) and seven 'Hautes Écoles Spécialisées' in 1998 aimed to enhance higher education and vocational training in specialized fields, integrating applied research and technology transfer into the Swiss higher education system.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The integration of applied research and technology transfer enhances the skills of the workforce, supporting innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The institutions have been established and are operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of these institutions significantly improves vocational training and education pathways, but does not constitute a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: A further step fostering labour market flexibility is the creation of the Universities of Applied Sciences in 1998, which is a new type of specialised university.

reforms.description: The Swiss government introduced a vocational baccalaureate in 1995 to enhance the dual education system, allowing apprentices to gain more general instruction and better employment prospects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform improves the skill set of apprentices, enhancing their employability and contributing to a more skilled workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The vocational baccalaureate has been successfully integrated into the education system.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the vocational baccalaureate represents a significant change in the education system, enhancing the dual education model.
reforms.source_quote: The vocational baccalaureate was introduced at upper secondary level between 1992 and 1995.

reforms.description: Switzerland will participate under the same conditions as any EU country in EU research programmes, allowing the participation of EU researchers in Swiss programmes, thereby enhancing collaboration and knowledge transfer in research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This participation fosters international collaboration, enhancing research capabilities and innovation potential in Switzerland.
reforms.status_evidence: Switzerland is actively participating in EU research programmes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in Switzerland's approach to international research collaboration.
reforms.source_quote: Switzerland will participate under the same conditions as any EU country in EU research programmes and will allow the participation of EU researchers in Swiss programmes.
